_comment: Cross-port parity classification for the 20 frozen goldens (7 biosphere + 13 station). Authored in Phase-7 Step 0 (P7.0) by inspecting each scenario's reachable evaluation graph (flows + resolver + schedule + scenario data) for transcendental ops. The `float_tier` is the tier for FLOAT TRAJECTORIES; the Tier-0 structural gate (see `_tier0`) is EXACT for every scenario regardless. Rule (advisor): classify by the ops the simulator EXECUTES, not the closed-form solution — a geometric contraction d_n=d0*(1-k*dt)^n is n sequential multiplies (IEEE basic ops, bit-identical across ports), NOT a pow() call, so it is Tier 1. Only a `math.{sin,cos,exp,log,pow,sqrt,tan}` / `**` in the per-step graph drops a scenario to Tier 2. `band`/`floor` are null until MEASURED (Tier-2 tolerances are measured, never derived). Step-3 (P7.3) measured the three standalone-sibling Tier-2 bands (power / power_self_discharge / thermal) via the propagated ±1-ULP transcendental SENSITIVITY (`measure_tier2_bands.py`), NOT the direct Rust-vs-Python deviation — which reads 0.0 on one machine because both call the same system libm (a same-libm artifact, not a cross-libm measurement). The band sits above that sensitivity; `test_crossport.py` re-measures and asserts band > sensitivity. Step-6 (P7.6) wired the whole parity suite into CI: the dedicated `crossport` job (Ubuntu, both toolchains) runs every `skipif cargo` comparison, so the bands are now enforced against glibc-Rust vs the UCRT-generated goldens — the repo's first real cross-libm gate (locally the deviation is 0.0 because Rust and CPython share one system libm; on the Linux runner the UCRT goldens make it a genuine cross-libm measurement). All 20 goldens pass their tier on that job. See docs/native-port-reference.md.

_tier0._comment: Universal structural/discrete gate — EXACT for EVERY scenario, all tiers. Enforced by compare.py independent of float_tier. This is the primary parity gate (the plan: 'Tier 0 is the primary gate'). The per-step-in-Rust conservation assertion and the events==()/rationed==0/period-class invariants are Rust-run-side and land with the engine in Steps 2+; the snapshot-visible subset is listed here.
exact_fields: per-stock domain / quantity / unit / kind / unclamped
summary period-class booleans (is_period_2, is_stationary) — the stability signature

_transcendental_sites._comment: The grep-confirmed transcendental call sites (Phase-7 Step 0 graph inspection). Any scenario whose reachable graph includes one of these is Tier 2.
_transcendental_sites.power/system.py:156: math.sin + math.pi — the half-sine solar schedule (Power)

goldens.evidence: crew/flows.py forced-linear depletion (OxygenConsumption/FoodMetabolism/WaterBalance), splits are params; grep of crew/{flows,system,scenario}.py clean of math.* and **. Advisor-flagged Tier-1 candidate — confirmed.

eclss/flows.py first-order linear (CO2Scrubber/Condenser/O2Makeup) + forced CrewMetabolism; geometric contraction is sequential multiplies, not pow(). grep of eclss/*.py clean. Tier-1 candidate — confirmed.

station/cabin.py wires crew CrewRespiration (station/flows.py, forced/linear) + eclss linear controls; NO biosphere in the graph (cabin-based, not greenhouse). grep of station/flows.py shows matches are all docstrings; code is transcendental-free. Tier-1 candidate — confirmed.

station/water.py adds donor-controlled WaterRecovery (linear k_rec, η-split params) atop the cabin; NO biosphere. grep clean. WaterRecovery makes water_store state-dependent (breaks forced RK4=Euler) but is still only *, +, -, / — bit-exact across ports. Tier-1 candidate — confirmed.

FvCB photosynthesis (exp/sqrt) + transpiration (exp) run every step for the plant/consumer chamber; weather trig for daylength. Frozen Euler. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

Same FvCB graph as consumer_chamber over a 15-yr horizon; divergence accumulates so the whole trajectory is Tier 2. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

FvCB photosynthesis/transpiration + weather trig every step; period-2 stability signature is Tier-0 (exact), the amounts are Tier 2. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

Same FvCB graph as perennial_chamber over 15 yr; Tier 2. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

Closed biosphere chamber; FvCB exp/sqrt + transpiration exp every step. Tier 2. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

Open-field single season; the same FvCB/transpiration/weather transcendentals. Tier 2. Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

Per-year peak-leaf / consumer-carbon series derived from the FvCB biosphere runs — numeric leaves are Tier 2. The is_period_2 booleans are the Tier-0 stability signature (exact). Step-4 (P7.4): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via propagated ±1-ULP transcendental sensitivity (worst 6.7e-14, canopy.exp/perennial-15yr — the contracting attractor barely amplifies), set to 1e-11 (~150x above).

The solar_schedule is a half-sine over the daylight window (math.sin/math.pi). Advisor-flagged: power is NOT Tier 1 despite the derived daily balance. Tier 2. Step-3 (P7.3) calibration: measured ±1-ULP sin-sensitivity propagates to 5.2e-15 (measure_tier2_bands.py); same-libm Rust-vs-Python deviation is 0.0. Band 1e-12 (~190x above measured) absorbs realistic multi-ULP cross-libm divergence while a real port defect still trips.

Reuses BOUNDED_SOC_SCENARIO's half-sine solar (inherits math.sin); the SelfDischarge leak itself is linear but the graph is already transcendental. Tier 2. Step-3 (P7.3) calibration: measured ±1-ULP sin-sensitivity propagates to 4.1e-15 (measure_tier2_bands.py); same-libm Rust-vs-Python deviation is 0.0. Band 1e-12.

RadiatorReject computes eps*sigma*A*(T^4 - T_space^4) via `**` every step — the nonlinear attractor. Must be op-for-op powf(4.0). Tier 2. Step-3 (P7.3) calibration: measured ±1-ULP t**4-sensitivity propagates to only 1.9e-16 (measure_tier2_bands.py; the contracting attractor damps it); same-libm Rust-vs-Python deviation is 0.0. Band 1e-12.

Power's half-sine solar dissipates into Thermal's T^4 radiator — both transcendentals in one graph. Tier 2. Step-5 (P7.5): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0); band measured via the propagated ±1-ULP sin+t**4 sensitivity of the coupled 7-day run (5.2e-15, measure_tier2_bands.measured_station_energy_sensitivity), set to 1e-12 (~190x above, the Step-3 power/thermal margin).

The full sealed assembly: biosphere FvCB + Thermal T^4 + all seams over a multi-year horizon. Tier 2 (the plan already calls this the 'Tier-2 combined-ledger' run). Step-5 (P7.5): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (same UCRT libm, max_rel_dev 0.0 over the full ~1.3M-substep run); shares BIOSPHERE_BAND 1e-11 (biosphere FvCB in the graph), justified by the cheap 7-day greenhouse canopy.exp measurement (2.7e-15) + the regulator-erasure argument (the scrubber/makeup hold the shared gas pools at setpoint between the once-daily biosphere lumps, so a 1-ULP nudge cannot amplify across master days). NOT swept on the sealed run itself (deliberate cost choice).

goldens.scenario: HEAT_CLOSURE_SCENARIO 15-yr energy stability signature (P6.7 Tier-1 energy)
goldens.evidence: node_peak_temp_k series comes from the T^4 radiator under half-sine solar — numeric leaves Tier 2. The is_stationary boolean is the Tier-0 stability signature (exact). NOTE: 'Tier-1 energy' in the scenario name is P6.7's internal *energy-vs-matter* tiering, NOT this cross-port float tier. Step-5 (P7.5): Rust emits the raw 15-yr node-heat series, Python folds temp/year-peaks/is_stationary (advisor #3, drift.py Python-side); bit-exact locally (max_rel_dev 0.0), is_stationary matches EXACTLY (Tier-0). Same Power→Thermal graph as station_state, so it shares its measured band 1e-12 (the T^4 attractor only damps the 5.2e-15 sensitivity further over 15 yr).

Couples the FvCB biosphere into the cabin; the plant's exp/sqrt are in the per-master-day graph. Tier 2 (transcendental-free cabin + transcendental biosphere = Tier 2, since divergence propagates). Step-5 (P7.5): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (max_rel_dev 0.0); shares BIOSPHERE_BAND 1e-11, measured via the 7-day greenhouse canopy.exp sensitivity (2.7e-15, measure_tier2_bands.measured_greenhouse_sensitivity).

Built on the greenhouse (biosphere in the graph); the Harvest flow itself is linear but the FvCB biosphere makes the scenario Tier 2. Step-5 (P7.5): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (max_rel_dev 0.0); shares BIOSPHERE_BAND 1e-11 (the 7-day greenhouse canopy.exp measurement, 2.7e-15).

The lamp forces the biosphere's PAR; the FvCB biosphere runs every master day. The Lamp/Power flows are forced-linear, but the biosphere transcendentals make it Tier 2. Step-5 (P7.5): Rust-vs-Python bit-exact locally (max_rel_dev 0.0); shares BIOSPHERE_BAND 1e-11 (the 7-day greenhouse canopy.exp measurement, 2.7e-15).
